name: claude-code
description: Claude Code automation, file management, report generation, web apps, invoice processing
topics: using Clot Code to automate workflows
organizing and cleaning up files with Clot Code
Content creation using cloud code and Notion
Turn your workflow into a web app

vocabulary.Clot code: A powerful AI assistant that can be used by non-technical people for automating tasks without coding.
vocabulary.warp terminal: A terminal specifically designed to work with AI agents, allowing users to write commands and plain English.
vocabulary.startup plan: A Clot Code subscription option suitable for minimal usage at $25.
vocabulary.max plan: A Clot Code subscription option for higher usage at $100 or $200.
vocabulary.cloud code: A tool for analyzing data, writing code to read and process files, and performing data analysis.
vocabulary.LS: Command used in cloud code to list files in the current directory.
vocabulary.CD: Command used in cloud code to change directories.
vocabulary.MCP: Model Context Protocol, an open, standardized way for AI applications like cloud code to communicate with external data sources.
vocabulary.invoice processing: The task of handling and organizing invoices.
vocabulary.multi-format support: Handling different file formats like PDF, Excel, images.

tips: Use the warp terminal to interact with Clot code as it is specifically designed to work with AI agents and reduces friction for non-technical users.
Install Clot code by running a curl command in your terminal.
Sign up for a Clot Code subscription if you plan to automate more than two or three tasks daily, starting from $25 for the startup plan up to $200 for the max plan.
Ensure that you are in the correct directory before using Clot code to clean up files; this allows Clot to access all relevant files within that folder.
Use the LS command to see what folders are available in your current directory.
Use the CD command to navigate into a specific folder.
Press shift plus tab to turn on except edits mode, which allows you to make changes without being prompted for each one.
Use Zapier MCP to quickly set up an MCP server for integrating with external applications like Notion.
Create a dedicated folder for raw and clean invoices to maintain organization.
Use the command 'pwd' in terminal to check the current working directory.
Ensure that you are in the right directory before running commands.
Ask Claude to process all different types of invoices into one single Excel file by extracting important details.
mistakes: Running Clot from your home or root directory instead of a specific project folder can limit its ability to access and manage files effectively.
Not subscribing to Clot Code if you plan to use it frequently, which could lead to limitations in functionality.
Not having the entire data in CSV or Excel files within the context window of chatgpd or cloud.ai
Not specifying technical details like language or tool when asking for a web app creation could lead to confusion.

Using cloud code to consolidate tech budget from multiple Excel files and identify year over year changes.
Creating a remote Notion MCP server using Zapier, granting access to specific databases and pages.
